tldr: Three items shipped this run (#28, #29, #30) via PR #71 (which bundled PRs #68/#69/#70). #28 (image alt text rewritten descriptive-not-titular across team profiles, condition pages, and service pages) closes G14. #29 (Hillmorton + Wigram + Addington + Halswell + Cashmere now appear in flowing intro copy on every service page in services.json, not just the footer line) closes G22. #30 (a 'Conditions we commonly treat' block on the osteopathy service page linking all six condition pages, plus a matching 'Common conditions we treat' section on the homepage) closes G23. Two further non-punch-list improvements also shipped: PR #72 made /book/ AI-citable with a ReserveAction schema + visible intro content (relevant for ChatGPT-driven booking flows now that Perplexity Health launched 2026-03-19 with first-party EHR integration), and PR #73 added explicit www → apex 301 redirects in netlify.toml so Google consolidates link equity onto the canonical apex domain. PR #74 added Kaylee Frost as a new practitioner (sixth team member).

Search visibility: 31 queries re-checked against live SERPs on 2026-05-15. Meridian still absent from every visible top 10 — same pattern as the previous five runs. Per the priority-decay rule, every checked query lost 10 points (e.g. 'osteopath christchurch' 60→50, 'osteopath halswell' 55→45, 'acupuncture christchurch' 55→45). Notable shifts vs 2026-05-08: 'dry needling halswell' regained an NZ player (Elite Physiotherapy Halswell back in top 5 vs gone last week) — the suburb+modality SERP is now partially contested again. 'osteopath addington' lost its only NZ player (Performance Plus disappeared) so the SERP is now fully UK-only — same hollow-SERP pattern as 'osteopath wigram' which remains uncontested. 'osteopath hillmorton' is STILL all-UK results — even after #29 added Hillmorton to flowing intro copy, Google hasn't re-indexed yet (re-check next run). The four condition pages from item #10 are now 4-5 weeks post-ship and STILL not surfacing for their target queries; #30 (cross-links from osteopathy intro + homepage) shipped 2026-05-10, so we'll know in 1-2 runs whether the internal-link surface accelerated indexing.

Backlog after this run: 8 items pending (#12, #13, #14, #15, #16, #17, #25, #31). Tier 1 → just #17 (dry-needling sub-page). Tier 2 → #12, #13, #25, #31. Tier 3 → #14, #15, #16. One new item added (#31, citation-ready opening pattern audit) addressing new gap G24. Closed gaps: G14 (alt text), G22 (suburb signals), G23 (cross-linking). Remaining gaps: G7 (only ACC landing remains under #25), G8 (no blog), G15 (no Reddit citations), G16 (no Western-framed dry-needling URL), G24 (intros don't open with a citation-ready 40–60 word direct answer).
alreadyShipped: llms.txt and llms-full.txt auto-generated from site data
Explicit AI-crawler allow-list in robots.txt (GPTBot, ClaudeBot, PerplexityBot, Googlebot-Extended, CCBot, anthropic-ai)
MedicalClinic + MedicalBusiness JSON-LD in base template with address, hours, priceRange, offer catalog
FAQPage JSON-LD on the homepage
Canonical URLs, OpenGraph, Twitter Card tags on every page
Auto-generated sitemap with lastmod
AggregateRating + Review schema (ratingValue 4.9, reviewCount 65, three real testimonials) nested under MedicalBusiness
Physician JSON-LD per team profile with sameAs to professional registers and visible 'Registered with …' block
Per-service MedicalTherapy / Service schema with provider, areaServed, hasOfferCatalog of specialties, and mainEntityOfPage
FAQPage JSON-LD on every service page (driven by faq arrays in services.json)
BreadcrumbList JSON-LD + visible breadcrumb strip on non-home pages
Visible 'Last updated' block on service and team pages, with dateModified in WebPage schema
sameAs block on clinic JSON-LD (Google Business Profile etc.)
Expanded llms.txt — Conditions-treated, Pricing-&-ACC, Common-questions sections
Homepage H1 reframed to 'Osteopathy, Acupuncture & Herbal Medicine in Christchurch'; practitioners section demoted to H2

/conditions/sciatica/, /conditions/headaches-migraines/, /conditions/pregnancy-back-pain/, /conditions/sports-injury-recovery/ — four condition Q→A pages with MedicalCondition + FAQPage schema, plus a What We Treat nav dropdown
ProfilePage + MedicalWebPage @graph schema on every team profile (alongside the Physician node), so each practitioner URL is its own retrievable AI-search entity
Hero subtitle and practitioners intro reframed to evidence-informed language (no longer says "holistic"); each line is now distinct rather than restating the H1
"Serving Hillmorton, Halswell, Hoon Hay, Cashmere, Addington, Riccarton, Sydenham, Wigram & surrounding Christchurch suburbs" — site-wide footer block + extended llms.txt Area-served line
Treats: chips on every team profile (in a What I Treat card), with Nina's row carrying a clickable pink-tinted pill linking to the new fertility page
/services/acupuncture/fertility/ sub-page — MedicalTherapy schema linking to the parent acupuncture #service, FAQPage on IVF timing / ACC status / Nina's credentials, hero + 3 specialty images
Site-wide sweep removing 'GP referral' from patient-facing copy (per the CLAUDE.md copy rule); condition CTA banners simplified to heading + booking button only

Google Business Profile services audit completed manually (item #21) — owner added Osteopathy, Medical acupuncture, Dry needling, Neck pain treatment, Headache & migraine treatment, Sports injury treatment, Pregnancy back pain, Paediatric osteopathy, Herbal medicine to GBP service list

/conditions/neck-pain/ — Q→A page with MedicalCondition + FAQPage schema, mirroring the four item-#10 pages; covers acute vs chronic, when to seek urgent care, ACC eligibility, links back to the osteopathy service (item #22, PR #63)

/conditions/back-pain/ — Q→A page with MedicalCondition + FAQPage schema; covers acute vs chronic, sciatica overlap, when to seek urgent care, ACC eligibility, links to /conditions/sciatica/ and the osteopathy service (item #23, PR #64)

<link rel="alternate" type="text/markdown" href="/llms.txt"> + the same for /llms-full.txt added in the HTML head — signals AI crawlers that a Markdown summary exists alongside the HTML (item #26, PR #62)

Physician schema upgraded to Schema.org v30 IndividualPhysician on every team profile, with practicesAt: { @id: '#clinic' } linking each practitioner to the MedicalClinic node — sharpens entity disambiguation for ChatGPT / Perplexity / AI Overviews (item #27, PR #65)

Image alt text rewritten descriptive-not-titular across team profiles (team-profile.njk now uses member.photoAlt with name fallback), condition pages, and service pages — each now describes what's visible plus relevant clinical context. Closes G14 (item #28, PR #70 / bundled in #71)

Hillmorton + Wigram + Addington + Halswell + Cashmere now appear in flowing intro copy on every service page in services.json (osteopathy, acupuncture, herbal medicine), not just the site-wide footer line. Closes G22 (item #29, PR #68 / bundled in #71)

'Conditions we commonly treat' block added to the osteopathy service page linking all six condition pages (back-pain, neck-pain, sciatica, headaches-migraines, pregnancy-back-pain, sports-injury-recovery), plus a matching 'Common conditions we treat' section on the homepage with high-intent button links — gives the condition pages an internal-link surface beyond the nav dropdown so Google can pass relevance signals to them. Closes G23 (item #30, PR #69 / bundled in #71)

/book/ page made AI-citable — added ReserveAction schema and visible intro content so ChatGPT / Perplexity / Gemini can recommend it as a booking action, not just an interstitial. Especially relevant after Perplexity Health launched 2026-03-19 with EHR integration, since AI assistants are increasingly handling the appointment-booking step (PR #72)

Explicit www → apex 301 redirects added in netlify.toml — Google now consolidates link equity onto the canonical meridianosteopathy.co.nz apex domain rather than splitting it between www and apex variants (PR #73)

gaps.title: No ACC landing page
gaps.why: Neck-pain and back-pain condition pages now ship (items #22, #23 closed). The remaining piece of G7 is a single indexable /acc/ URL — osteo.co.nz still owns 'osteopath acc christchurch' and we have ACC mentions scattered across the site but no canonical landing. Scoped under item #25.

gaps.title: No blog / editorial content
gaps.why: Freshness cliff at ~3 months. April-2026 industry data: 76.4% of ChatGPT's top-cited pages were updated within 30 days, 50% of Perplexity citations come from content less than 13 weeks old. osteopath-christchurch.com is winning headache/migraine/cranial/baby-osteo queries on blog-post velocity — 1 short post per condition is enough to claim those niches. MK Osteopathy is doing the same for TMJ. May-2026 BrightEdge data confirms the pattern is hardening: AI Overviews now trigger on 80%+ of healthcare queries, with cited brands earning 35% more clicks and AI visitors converting 4.4× higher than organic.

gaps.title: No off-site citation strategy
gaps.why: Reddit appears in ~47% of Perplexity citations and ~5% of ChatGPT citations (May 2026 CMSWire/Conductor data). Reddit is the second most visible website in Google search results in 2026 (only Wikipedia ahead) and is now the most-cited domain across all major AI models — 450% growth in AI-overview citations year-on-year. Item #15 still applies.

gaps.title: No Western-framed dry-needling landing URL
gaps.why: The gap isn't that the acupuncture page leads with TCM (that's correct framing for our existing patient base), it's that we have NO indexable URL targeting the Western-medical / dry-needling intent at all. 'dry needling christchurch' is still 100% physio-owned (Fixed Physio, Muscle People, Physiosouth, Physio NZ, Koru Therapies). Item #17 adds a sibling sub-page (mirror of /services/acupuncture/fertility/) rather than reframing the parent page.

gaps.title: Service + condition intros don't open with a citation-ready 40–60 word direct answer
gaps.why: May 2026 healthcare AEO/GEO research is consistent on this: ChatGPT, Perplexity and Google AI Overviews preferentially cite content that opens with a direct, concise answer to the query intent in the first 40-60 words, then expands with supporting context. Our current intros open with topic-framing rather than answer-leading copy — e.g. /conditions/back-pain/ opens 'Back pain is the most common reason people see an osteopath, and one of the most common reasons people miss work, sleep, sport, and time with their families.' Informative, but not answer-leading. A citation-ready rewrite would open 'Back pain treatment at Meridian Osteopathy in Christchurch combines hands-on assessment, manual therapy, and movement-based recovery — most cases respond well to 3–6 sessions, ACC-funded, no referral required.' Then the existing prose can continue. Same pattern applies to every service intro. Addressed by item #31.

items.title: Add speakable blocks on service intros
items.body: Add schema.org speakable specification on service intro paragraphs for voice assistants / Gemini Live.

items.title: Auto-fetch Google Business Profile reviews at build time
items.body: So reviewCount / ratingValue stay current without manual edits. Build script hits Places API with a 24h cache. Reinforced this week: post-March-2026 Google update weights review recency more heavily than raw count.

items.title: Start a blog (2 posts/month)
items.body: Short evidence-based articles per condition. Each post: author byline with Physician schema, datePublished, dateModified, 3–5 FAQs. Ahrefs analysis of 17M AI citations (April 2026) found AI-cited content is 25.7% fresher than traditional Google organic results — 76.4% of ChatGPT's top-cited pages were updated within the last 30 days. Freshness compounds. Confirmed again 2026-05-15: osteopath-christchurch.com still owns headaches/cranial/baby/teeth-grinding queries on the strength of one short blog post per condition; MK Osteopathy still owns the TMJ slot the same way. Per BrightEdge May-2026 data, AI Overviews now trigger on 80%+ of healthcare queries — the freshness signal feeds directly into AI Overview citation eligibility.

items.title: Seed Reddit / community brand mentions
items.body: Genuine, non-spammy answers on r/newzealand / r/chch / r/AskNZ threads about osteopathy, ACC claims, back pain. Reddit is now ~47% of Perplexity citations and ~5% of ChatGPT citations across major AI platforms (May 2026 Conductor / CMSWire data) and the second most visible site in Google results overall (Wikipedia first). 90/10 rule: 90% non-promotional, 2–4 weeks of karma-building before any mention of Meridian.

items.title: Encourage monthly Google reviews nudge
items.body: Post-consultation email nudge via Resend (already wired). AI engines prefer reviews under 12 months old. Reinforced 2026-05-02: Google's March 2026 core update now weights review recency AND owner response rate more heavily than raw count. Healthcare best-practice = at least 1 new review/week.

items.title: Add /services/acupuncture/dry-needling/ sub-page (Western framing) — keep main acupuncture page TCM-first
items.body: REVISED 2026-05-10 per owner feedback: the original framing (reframe the main acupuncture page to lead with Western medical / dry needling) would alienate existing TCM patients who book Nina specifically for Traditional Chinese Medicine. Instead, mirror the pattern already shipped at /services/acupuncture/fertility/: keep the parent /services/medical-acupuncture/ page TCM-first as it is today, and add a sibling sub-page /services/acupuncture/dry-needling/ that leads with 'Medical acupuncture / dry needling — Western evidence-based technique for trigger-point and musculoskeletal pain'. Two indexable URLs, each surfacing for its own intent, no compromise on TCM identity. Sub-page should: (a) name Nina's Postgraduate Certificate in Health Science (Acupuncture) in the first paragraph — exact credential ChatGPT told users to look for; (b) explicitly mention Halswell so Google has local content to surface (the Halswell+modality SERP is empty of NZ content most weeks — Elite Physio Halswell flickered in/out across the last 3 runs); (c) carry a MedicalTherapy schema linking back to the parent acupuncture #service; (d) include a FAQPage block on dry-needling-vs-acupuncture, ACC status, and what to expect. Why this is now the better shape: the existing fertility sub-page already proves the pattern works in our build, and 'dry needling christchurch' is still 100% physio-owned (Muscle People, Fixed Physio, Physiosouth, Physio NZ, Koru Therapies) so a dedicated landing URL is the smallest defensible move. Effort stays S — small templated page mirroring the fertility sub-page.

items.title: Not ranking for 'osteopath acc christchurch' — osteo.co.nz owns it
items.body: osteo.co.nz has a dedicated ACC landing page that wins this query (confirmed again 2026-05-15). We already have the ACC pricing row in services.json and scattered ACC mentions, but no single indexable /acc/ URL. Build a short page (~500 words) explaining ACC-funded treatment at Meridian, the current ACC rate, no referral required, how to lodge a claim, and link out to both service pages and a booking CTA. Low-effort content fix.

items.title: Rewrite condition + service intros with a citation-ready 40–60 word direct answer first
items.body: May 2026 healthcare AEO/GEO research (ALM Corp, Conductor, BrightEdge, RankVed, Sapt) converges on the same opening pattern for AI-citation-eligible content: lead with a direct, concise answer to the query intent in the first 40-60 words, then expand with supporting context. AI Overviews now trigger on 80%+ of healthcare queries and cited brands earn 35% more clicks (4.4× higher conversion vs organic). Our intros currently open with topic-framing — sample: /conditions/back-pain/ opens 'Back pain is the most common reason people see an osteopath, and one of the most common reasons people miss work, sleep, sport, and time with their families.' That's good prose but it's not answer-leading. The fix is small: rewrite the first 40-60 words of every condition intro AND every service intro in services.json to lead with 'X treatment at Meridian Osteopathy in Christchurch …' format, then keep the existing copy as the second paragraph onward. Same approach for /conditions/* (back-pain, neck-pain, sciatica, headaches-migraines, pregnancy-back-pain, sports-injury-recovery) and /services/* (osteopathy, medical-acupuncture, herbal-medicine, acupuncture/fertility). 10 intros × ~30 words rewritten = under an hour of editor time. Keeps the existing evidence-informed prose intact; just front-loads the answer.
